response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with roughly 15+ years of engineering experience and a genuine, sustained interest in testing infrastructure and developer tooling (test runners, assertion libraries, CI setup, Selenium training, MCP code generators). However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing people, owning a team roadmap, or leading organizational change. The developer-tools work is also largely open-source side projects rather than professional ownership of build systems or engineering-productivity infrastructure, making the fit for a full-time engineering manager position weak despite solid technical depth.
strengths: Deep, long-running involvement in testing and developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI/dev-ops setup, Selenium course, MCP code generators)
15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, including staff/senior roles at large tech companies
Mobile platform exposure through iOS and Apple TV app ownership and a mobile device orchestration tool
concerns: No people-management or formal team-leadership experience, which is a core minimum requirement for this role
Developer-tools and testing-infrastructure work is mostly personal open-source projects rather than professional build-system or eng-productivity ownership
No demonstrated strategic roadmap planning or organizational-change leadership; resume also contains confusing overlapping entries that raise accuracy questions

key_factors.factor: No roadmap or organizational leadership track record
